subject: Where does System.out.println actually print?
body: Beginner question — when I run my Main.java in VS Code I see output in the terminal, but when I push to my handout repo I'm not sure where it shows up. Is it in the autograder logs?

replies.body: Yes, the autograder captures stdout. After your run finishes, click into the submission and look at the 'Output' tab on each test.
Took me forever to figure this out too lol, the output tab is hidden behind the chevron on each test row.

subject: Compile error: cannot find symbol Println
body: I'm getting `cannot find symbol: method Println(String)` even though I copied the code from the slides. Help?

replies.body: Java is case-sensitive — it's `println` (lowercase p), not `Println`.
+1 to above. The slides have an autocorrect issue, sorry about that.

subject: off-by-one in the FizzBuzz lab
body: My FizzBuzz prints 1 through 99 instead of 1 through 100. I have `for (int i = 1; i < 100; i++)`. What am I missing?

replies.body: `<` stops one short. Use `<=` if you want to include 100.

subject: when to use while vs for
body: Is there a rule of thumb for when to reach for `while` instead of `for`? Both seem to do the same thing in the examples we covered.

replies.body: Rule of thumb I use: `for` when I know the count up front, `while` when I'm looping until something becomes true (user input, file end, etc.).
Also `for` makes the iteration variable scope smaller which is usually nicer.

subject: ConcurrentModificationException when removing in a loop

replies.body: Can't modify a collection while iterating with an enhanced for. Use an Iterator and call `it.remove()`, or use `removeIf(n -> n < 0)`.
removeIf is so clean, didn't know about it until now thx

subject: ArrayList vs LinkedList — does it matter for HW3?
body: Does the spec require an ArrayList specifically or could I use a LinkedList? Functionally they seem the same.

replies.body: Spec asks for ArrayList. They both implement List but ArrayList is what the test cases instantiate.

subject: StackOverflowError on the Fibonacci problem
body: My recursive fib(40) hangs forever and eventually crashes. Is recursion just slow or am I doing something wrong?

replies.body: Naive recursive fib is exponential — fib(40) makes about a billion calls. Look up memoization for next week's lecture.
Also if it's literally hanging and not crashing, double-check your base case isn't off.

subject: Tracing recursion by hand is hurting my brain
body: I get the textbook examples but the moment I try to write my own recursive method I freeze. Any advice on the mental model?

replies.body: Try the 'leap of faith' approach: assume the recursive call works correctly for smaller inputs, then ask what to do with that result. Don't trace the whole stack in your head.
Visualizer at pythontutor.com supports Java now, helped me a ton.

subject: Can a class extend two parents?
body: I want Square to inherit from both Shape and Drawable. Java won't let me say `extends Shape, Drawable`.

replies.body: Java does single inheritance for classes. Make Drawable an interface and `implements Drawable` alongside `extends Shape`.

subject: @Override is making my code red
body: I added @Override to my toString and now IntelliJ is yelling at me. The method works fine without it.

replies.body: Check the spelling — usually that means you wrote `toSTring` or similar and aren't actually overriding the superclass method.

subject: Are partner submissions allowed for HW3?
body: The spec for HW3 doesn't say anything about pairs. Can two of us submit together?

replies.body: HW3 is individual. Labs are pair-allowed, but homeworks aren't.

subject: Late tokens — can I get them back?
body: I burned a late token because GitHub Actions was down on Sunday night. Any chance of a refund?

replies.body: Please file an extension request from your submission page and we'll review.

subject: Office hours moved this week?
body: Calendar shows Wed OH cancelled. Is there a make-up session?

replies.body: Yes — moving to Thursday 4pm in the same room. Updated the course calendar.

subject: Required textbook?
body: Is the Sedgewick book required or just recommended? It's pretty expensive.

replies.body: Recommended. All required reading is on the course site.
Library has 3 copies on reserve fwiw.

subject: me debugging at 3am
body: println statements everywhere. so many printlns. why did i choose this major.

replies.body: wait til you discover the actual debugger lol
println debugging is real debugging, fight me

subject: When IntelliJ suggests a fix and it actually works
body: best feeling. better than coffee. better than weekends.

replies.body: Until you accept the fix and 12 new errors appear in other files.

subject: OH queue etiquette — what counts as 'stuck'?
body: I've been on the same problem for 2 hours. Should I hop in the queue or keep pushing?

replies.body: 2 hours is plenty. Come to OH. We'd rather unstick you in 10 min than have you spin for another 2.

subject: Will the midterm cover recursion?
body: Just want to plan study time appropriately.

replies.body: Yes — through HW5 material, which includes recursion.

subject: Lab Friday is in person this week
body: Reminder: Friday's lab will meet in 410W as normal. We'll skip the Zoom hybrid session.

subject: Study group for midterm?
body: Anyone interested in a study group this Sunday at the library? I'll bring practice problems.

replies.body: I'm in! Snell 2nd floor?
+1, what time?
